title: I use TradingNews AI’s API to get data for prediction market trading, and it feels pretty effective.
author: bjxxjj
selftext:
**Has anyone tried using a real-time news API for prediction market trading?**

Over the past few weeks, I’ve been testing a strategy where I feed real-time financial news into my Polymarket trading setup, and the results have been better than expected.

Here’s how I’m doing it: I use the TradingNews API to get breaking-level news and sentiment scores, then compare that with the current odds on Polymarket to find markets where pricing hasn’t reacted yet.

A real example from last week: when the FOMC decision came out, the API pushed the update almost instantly and tagged it as breaking + negative sentiment. At that moment, the odds on a Polymarket rate-hike contract hadn’t moved yet. I entered the trade and closed it 20 minutes later.

My strategy:

1. Only look at breaking-level news; ignore everything else
2. Check whether the sentiment direction conflicts with current market pricing
3. If there’s a mismatch, enter with a small position

Is anyone else doing something similar? Most of the strategies I’ve seen are purely odds-based or technical, and very few people seem to be plugging in real-time news feeds.

comments.author: pluxrt90
comments.body:
I’ve done something similar but with options trading instead. I scrape world and us news from the internet and run it through a pipeline of agents to classify and analyze them. Then I have a manger decide to call or put and use a deterministic option contract from there. 

Your idea of using real time news for predication markets might be smart, but also a lot of insider trading happens and if it’s something political/world news anything can really happen. As long as it’s used to get the actual decisions before the market close it might be a good idea, though possibly short lived. 

So far for options trading it’s worked well and worked horribly the next, so it feels kinda like gambling. I am still working on it, mainly the risk management as I am new to options myself. If you’d like to discuss more about these research type trading bots I’d love to get some more perspectives. It feels like I can’t find almost anything on them but I’m almost certain I’m not the first with this idea.
GitHub repo if your interested and want to take a look!: https://github.com/GeorgeStatho/agentic-trading-research

replies.author: grimmjoww1983
replies.body: I feel is that most of the time news by the time it comes out is already priced in

comments.author: Successful-Limit9444
comments.body: Interesting approach. I've been doing something similar but with whale flow data instead of news. Watching where high-volume wallets move on Polymarket before news drops has been surprisingly reliable. predterminal.com has a free scanner that tracks large wallet movements across all Polymarket and Kalshi markets in real time. The smart money vs crowd divergence feature is also worth checking out

comments.author: Quirky-Bit-6813
comments.body: Can I check out your agent?
